name: Israel
summary: Day 46. IDF raided Bint Jbeil hospital (killed ~20 Hezbollah fighters), cut roads, used white phosphorus. Two IDF soldiers wounded April 13. IDF Chief Zamir ordered 'heightened state of readiness' after Islamabad talks failure. Israel-Lebanon ambassador-level talks still on for Tuesday. Haaretz: 'Trump considering limited strikes on Iran.' Military tempo continues unabated — Lebanon operations intensifying while diplomatic track at State Dept proceeds in parallel.

signals.content: Israel's Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed 'preemptive' strike on Iran. Declared nationwide state of emergency. First phase planned for 4 days targeting senior leadership including Khamenei.
IDF launched ground incursion into southern Lebanon against Hezbollah. Described as 'forward defence' measure. Separate from Iran air campaign.
US struck Kharg Island. Israel struck nuclear sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan) multiple times. Fourth targeted attack on nuclear facilities.
Israel struck South Pars gas field (world's largest). Major escalation into energy infrastructure. Trump denied advance knowledge.
Iran launched 5 missile salvos at Jerusalem and northern Israel within one hour. Struck towns near Dimona nuclear site, 180+ wounded.

Israel assassinated IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri at Bandar Abbas March 26 — confirmed by IDF and CENTCOM. Netanyahu hailed killing of 'the man who led closure of Hormuz.' Defense Minister Katz vowed to 'intensify and expand' attacks. Separately, first Houthi ballistic missile intercepted over Beersheba March 28 — no casualties. (Sources: IDF, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, CNN, Jerusalem Post)

Iranian missiles struck Tel Aviv commercial street — Vyacheslav Vidmant (52) killed, 11+ injured, 8 impact sites including a university. Extensive building damage within 150m radius. Houthis launched two separate attacks on Israel within 24 hours — both intercepted, demonstrating sustained multi-front bombardment (Iran direct + Houthi from Yemen + Hezbollah from Lebanon). IDF killed 2 top Hezbollah communications unit members in Beirut overnight. CENTCOM struck Houthi positions in Yemen in response to Israel attacks. Israel expanding ground operations in southern Lebanon. (Sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, CNN, Defense News)

IDF confirmed targeting Malek Ashtar University and Imam Hossein University (IRGC-linked military research). IDF launched 'widespread' strikes across Tehran Saturday evening — dozens of infrastructure targets. Iran army targeted Haifa military aerospace complex and Ben Gurion airport fuel storage. Iran fired 6 missile salvos at Israel Saturday. Multi-front bombardment continues: Iran direct + Houthi from Yemen + Hezbollah from Lebanon. (Sources: IDF statement, CNN, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel)

EVENING UPDATE: Fire reported at Bazan oil refinery in Haifa following Iranian missile attack (Al Jazeera liveblog). Bazan is Israel's largest oil refinery. If confirmed, significant hit to Israel's energy infrastructure. Israel intercepted drones from Yemen (Houthi). Limited details on Bazan damage extent. Trump told Times of Israel that killing Iran's leaders 'truly is regime change' and deal 'could be soon.' (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, Times of Israel)

Three major developments: (1) IDF struck 170 targets 'throughout Tehran' in 24 hours including weapons production sites. Isfahan nuclear site struck — houses ~540 kg 60%-enriched uranium. (2) Iran missiles continued hitting central Israel, 8 lightly hurt. Missile rate 10-15/day vs 90/day Day 1. (3) Hezbollah unleashed 600+ attacks in 24 hours — doubling prior single-day high (~300). 4 IDF soldiers killed in southern Lebanon. Israel scaling back top missile defenses due to interceptor depletion (JPost). Haaretz: Netanyahu ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon.' Arms production tripled. Jewish opposition more than doubled (4%→11.5%), 'strongly support' down 74%→68%. WSJ exit report may accelerate Netanyahu's strikes. CONFIRMED (multiple independent sources).

Israel accelerating military campaign alongside peace signals: IDF struck 170 targets 'throughout Tehran' in 24 hours. Unnamed Israeli official: 'no intention to scale back before talks.' Haaretz reported Netanyahu ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon' — suggesting Israel is maximizing military damage while the diplomatic window exists. This diverges sharply from Trump's '2-3 weeks' exit timeline and Pezeshkian's peace overture. Israel pursuing its own objectives independent of US diplomatic posturing. The gap: Trump says 'great progress,' Israel says 'no scale-back' — allies not coordinating exit strategy. If ceasefire arrives, Israel wants maximum damage already inflicted. (Sources: IDF, Reuters, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Business Standard)

Israel killed Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem — commander of Hezbollah's southern front — in Beirut airstrike April 1. Seven killed, 26 wounded. Most senior Hezbollah kill since war began March 2. Hezbollah confirmed the death. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Hezbollah statement)

IDF confirmed '170 targets in 24 hours throughout Tehran' on April 1-2. Unnamed Israeli official: 'no intention to scale back' before talks. Netanyahu previously ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon' per WSJ. Targeting now includes Iranian steel plants (Mobarakeh, Khouzestan) and pharmaceutical facilities — new category of economic infrastructure destruction. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CBS, NPR)

IDF entering 'decisive phase': all high-priority pre-war targets likely eliminated by April 3 (Wednesday). 60-70% of identified sites damaged. IDF planning 3 MORE weeks of operations to systematically degrade Iran's defense industry. Target categories: critical (ballistic missile, nuclear-linked), essential (broader military-industrial network), satellite/space capabilities. Iran fired missiles at Israel — homes and cars damaged. Total casualty toll in Iran: 2,076 killed, 26,500 wounded since Feb 28. (Sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Time)

IDF struck 200+ Iranian targets and 140+ Hezbollah sites as of April 5. Total: 4,000+ targets, 10,000+ components (IDF BG Defrin). 60-70% of pre-war high-priority targets damaged. 3+ weeks more operations planned. In Lebanon: killed 2 girls, wounded 40 near Tyre April 4. Hospital damaged. IDF soldier killed by friendly fire. Lebanon total: 1,001 killed, 2,584 wounded. (Sources: ANI, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera)

CONFIRMED: Haifa missile strike — 2 dead recovered from rubble, 2 still trapped. IDF assessed CLUSTER BOMB WARHEAD — significant escalation in warhead type. Four others wounded. Rescue operation ongoing with Haifa Fire & Rescue, IDF Home Front Command, Magen David Adom. ~170 ballistic missiles launched in April 4-5 window — 6 on Friday, 8 on Saturday. Half targeting northern Israel, 5 toward center, one each south and Jerusalem. Separate Tel Aviv residential strike killed civilian woman in forties, injured 27. BAZAN refinery in Haifa hit in same salvo. Total war hospital evacuations: 6,833.

Lebanon strikes escalating: 15+ killed April 5-6. Beirut Jnah: 5 killed, 52 wounded (~100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital). Ain Saadeh: 3 killed. Kfar Hatta: 7 killed including 4-year-old girl. IDF Chief Lt. Gen. Zamir visited troops in southern Lebanon, pledged to 'intensify strikes.' Masnaa border crossing with Syria FORCED CLOSED after Israeli threat — civilians now queuing at Qaa northern crossing. Lebanon total: 1,460+ killed (Health Ministry).

CLAIMED (Hezbollah): Cruise missile fired at Israeli warship 68nm off Lebanese coast at midnight April 6. Hezbollah claims 'direct hit confirmed' after hours of surveillance. FIRST anti-ship cruise missile claim by Hezbollah since war began. IDF told AFP: 'We are not aware of it.' Unverified videos circulating. If confirmed, major capability escalation — C4ISR sophistication at 68nm range. IDF denial should not be treated as final given F-15 shootdown was eventually confirmed after initial ambiguity.

Israel struck Iran's South Pars petrochemical plant (largest, half of petrochemical output) and Mahshahr petrochemical complex (Fajr 1/2 utility plants destroyed, all production shut) — first sustained strike campaign directly targeting Iran's energy/industrial infrastructure. Defense Minister Katz confirmed. Also struck three Tehran airports (dozens of helicopters/aircraft destroyed). IRGC Intelligence Chief Brigadier General Khademi killed in precision strike — most significant intelligence decapitation since Tangsiri. Quds Force commander Bagheri also eliminated. IDF Northern Command chief Milo admitted Israel overestimated damage to Hezbollah — 'significant gap' between assessed and actual capabilities. Hezbollah retains hundreds of launchers and tens of thousands of rockets. IDF deems militarily disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic' — new objective: 2-3km security zone. Haaretz: IDF 'stretched to breaking point.' Haifa cluster bomb final count: 4 dead (Gershovich family + partner). 210+ Hezbollah missiles into Israel since March 2, ~100 during Passover. (Sources: Euronews, i24News, Military.com, IDF, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, FDD)

SOUTH PARS PETROCHEMICAL HIT FOR SECOND TIME + IRGC INTEL CHIEF DECAPITATION [CONFIRMED]. April 6 strike on the petrochemical plant inside South Pars natural gas field (world's largest, shared with Qatar). Defense Minister Katz on the record: 'a powerful strike on the largest petrochemical facility in Iran' — plant responsible for HALF Iran's petrochem output. Combined with prior week strike on #2 petrochem site, ~85% of Iran's petrochemical production now offline. Same operation killed Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi (IRGC Intel Chief — most senior IRGC intel loss of the war) and Asghar Bakeri (Quds Force undercover unit leader). Israel choosing to kill IRGC intel chief DURING ceasefire mediation is itself evidence of intent: Israel does not believe a deal will hold and is making one impossible.

NETANYAHU PHONED TRUMP WARNING AGAINST CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. Sunday April 5, Netanyahu phoned Trump ostensibly to congratulate on F-15 crew rescue. Per Israeli reporting (Haaretz, Times of Israel), he in fact warned Trump that 'a ceasefire carries significant risks' and against agreeing to a truce at this stage. Trump reportedly told Netanyahu a truce remains possible IF US demands are met by Iran but 'stressed that he would not give up on his demand that Tehran hand over all of its enriched uranium and agree not to resume enrichment.' First public US confirmation that the maximalist nuclear demand remains the US ceiling. US-Israel alignment visibly fraying: Netanyahu wants escalation; Trump wants a deal but on terms Iran will never accept. The Israel-US split is the critical fault line.

HAARETZ 'ISRAELI POLITICAL SOURCE': US-Iran talks likely to fail; Israel prepares 'extensive attack.' Source almost certainly a Netanyahu war cabinet member. Confidence that talks will fail tracks the South Pars decapitation timing — Israel has war-gamed the ceasefire collapse and is staging force for an operation 'larger than current operations.' Tripartite salvo (Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis) intercepted by IDF April 6. Beirut southern suburbs (dahiya) and Christian town of Ain Saadeh hit overnight. Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir announced widening of Operation Northern Arrows: 'The damage to Hezbollah will intensify.'

EVENING UPDATE: IDF executed pre-deadline 'wide-scale wave' — dozens of infrastructure sites struck hours before 8 PM ET. Three Tehran airports struck (dozens of helicopters/aircraft destroyed). Shiraz Marvdasht petrochemical complex hit. Persian-language railway warning issued to all Iran. Multiple bridges struck. 150+ aircraft involved. SABIC Jubail fire — missile debris fell near Saudi facilities during Israeli operations, most significant Saudi energy infrastructure attack of war. Rafi-Nia synagogue in Tehran 'completely destroyed' — Iran will use for propaganda. IAEA 'deeply concerned' by strikes near Bushehr — 4th incident, 1 staff killed. 98th Division deployed to southern Lebanon for 'forward defensive posture.' 91st Division killed Hezbollah anti-tank operators. Netanyahu phoned Trump warning against ceasefire (REPORTED, Haaretz). Haaretz 'Israeli political source': US-Iran talks likely to fail; Israel prepares 'extensive attack.' (Sources: ABC News, Al Jazeera, Arab News, Middle East Eye, Haaretz, Times of Israel)

ISRAEL BACKS BILATERAL US-IRAN CEASEFIRE — BUT EXCLUDES LEBANON [CONFIRMED]. Netanyahu's position evolved within 24 hours: he initially urged Trump NOT to agree to the ceasefire (his Sunday April 5 phone call to Trump warned against it), then BACKED the two-week pause after Trump's announcement, but explicitly stated the deal 'does not include Lebanon' and reaffirmed continued operations against Hezbollah. Earlier, Netanyahu reportedly told senior officials Saturday that Hezbollah operations 'would not be halted even if Iran and the United States reached a ceasefire.' This Lebanon exclusion is the structural risk to the entire deal: Iran's 10-point plan demanded 'end of war across all fronts' including Hezbollah, Houthis, and Iraqi PMF. The bilateral US-Iran ceasefire as structured does not deliver this. If Israel strikes Beirut on Day 1 of negotiations, Iran's hardliners will frame the deal as bad-faith and pressure to withdraw. The IDF struck a Christian mountain town (Ain Saadeh) east of Beirut on April 6, killing a prominent anti-Hezbollah politician and his wife. Operations continue.

IDF executed 'most intense day of strikes since Day One' on April 7 BEFORE the ceasefire — South Pars #2 petrochemical, three Tehran airports, Shiraz petrochemical complex, bridges, railways, plus the Khademi assassination [CONFIRMED]. Israeli operational objectives were largely achieved: ~85% Iranian petrochemical destruction, IRGC intelligence chief eliminated, Tehran airports degraded. Israel can accept the 2-week pause without giving up Hezbollah front. The Iranian cluster munition retaliation hit ~50 sites in central Israel, 6 wounded.

Israel launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' on April 8 — 50 IAF fighter jets struck 100 Hezbollah targets across Lebanon in 10 minutes using approximately 160 munitions. Central Beirut struck WITHOUT prior warning. At least 254 killed, 1,165 wounded — the single deadliest day of the 2026 Lebanon war. Defense Minister Katz: 'Today Hezbollah has suffered its heaviest blow since the 2024 beeper operation.' This was the predicted failure mode flagged in the April 8 brief: 'Any IDF strike on Beirut during Friday Islamabad talks gives Iran's hardliners cause to claim bad faith.' It happened within 18 hours, not even waiting for Friday.

Netanyahu explicitly stated ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon,' reaffirmed continued operations against Hezbollah. Trump called Lebanon a 'separate skirmish.' Pakistan PM Sharif directly contradicted both, stating ceasefire covers 'everywhere including Lebanon' citing Iran's 10-point plan. This creates a three-way contradiction on ceasefire scope that is the structural fault line for Friday Islamabad talks.

Day 43. Israel struck ADDITIONAL targets in Lebanon on April 9 despite ceasefire — putting the truce 'in further jeopardy.' Operation Eternal Darkness toll revised UP: 254-300+ killed, 1,150 wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry). Hezbollah retaliated with rockets into northern Israel on April 9. Netanyahu authorized direct negotiations with Lebanon at State Department starting next week (Axios). Vance's offer to 'restrain Lebanon strikes during talks' appears not honored given April 9 continuation. France (Macron) entered scope dispute saying Lebanon 'fully included' — making it 3-vs-2 against US/Israel position. Netanyahu separately stated enriched uranium 'will be removed by agreement or in resumed fighting.' (Source: PBS, Axios, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel)

Day 44. Israel escalated 'negotiate while bombing' strategy. Nabatieh strike killed 13 Lebanese State Security personnel at government building — NOT Hezbollah, these are Lebanese government security forces. Largest attack on Nabatieh since war began. Also struck residential areas in Aita al-Shaab, al-Majadel (Tyre district), and Hanine. HRW reported Israeli strikes killed hundreds and damaged vital bridge. Hezbollah ESCALATED dramatically in response: 100+ rockets since ceasefire, 70 on April 10. Most significant: struck Ashdod Naval Base in central Israel (~200km from Lebanese border) — if confirmed, qualitative capability escalation from border harassment to strategic depth. Also struck Haifa (sirens across city), Kiryat Shmona (fire, no warning siren), Karmiel (4-month-old infant and 4 children injured), and Nahariya. Haaretz military correspondents report systemic gap between political rhetoric and military reality: IDF intelligence assesses Hezbollah maintains 'solid military hold in southern Lebanon,' IDF 'not planning to advance deeper' (halted 10km south of Litani), senior officers say 'disarming Hezbollah can only be done by Lebanon,' IDF 'stretched to breaking point.' Trump told Israeli journalist he instructed Netanyahu to keep operations 'low key' to avoid derailing Pakistan talks — a shift from 'not included' to implicit acknowledgment that Lebanon violence threatens the talks.

Day 45. Israeli strikes killed at least 18 people across southern Lebanon on April 11: at least 8 killed in a village near Sidon, and at least 10 (including 3 emergency workers) killed in Nabatieh district. IDF said it struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in 24 hours. THREE diplomatic developments: (1) Israel-Lebanon talks set for Tuesday at State Department between Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter and Lebanese counterpart — but Leiter explicitly stated Israel 'refused to discuss a ceasefire with the Hezbollah terrorist organization.' (2) Lebanon PM Nawaf Salam CANCELLED his planned Washington trip, citing need to 'safeguard the security of the Lebanese people and their unity' as Hezbollah supporters rallied in Beirut against negotiations. Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah rejected direct talks as violating Lebanon's constitutional framework. (3) Ghalibaf named Lebanon ceasefire as one of Iran's preconditions for any Islamabad deal — making Lebanon the structural link between the bilateral US-Iran track and the broader regional conflict. Total Lebanon toll since March 2: 2,020 killed, 6,436 wounded.

AFTERNOON UPDATE: Israel continued striking southern Lebanon overnight April 11-12, killing at least 11 in addition to the 13 security personnel in Nabatieh (death toll revised up). IDF confirmed striking "loaded and ready-to-launch rocket launcher" in Jouaiya. Operational tempo 200+ targets in 24 hours continues. Two rockets and one drone launched from Lebanon toward Israel -- sirens in Upper Galilee. Tuesday DC talks still set at ambassador level (downgraded from PM) after Salam cancelled Washington trip. Israel rejects discussing ceasefire with Hezbollah -- focusing on military degradation while keeping diplomatic appearance. Lebanon is where Israel has operational agency; Islamabad was background noise. (Sources: IDF statement, Al Jazeera, The National)

IDF raided Bint Jbeil hospital — killed ~20 Hezbollah fighters. Cut roads in southern Lebanon, used white phosphorus. Two IDF soldiers wounded April 13. IDF Chief Zamir ordered 'heightened state of readiness' after Islamabad talks failure. Israel-Lebanon ambassador-level talks still on for Tuesday at State Dept. Haaretz: 'Trump considering limited strikes on Iran' — if confirmed, major escalation of US direct involvement.

edges.context: Day 44. Nabatieh 13 State Security killed + Hezbollah reached Ashdod (200km). Trump told Netanyahu 'low key' — implicit acknowledgment Lebanon violence threatens Islamabad talks. Ghalibaf set Lebanon ceasefire as Islamabad precondition, directly citing Israeli strikes. Israel's 'negotiate while bombing' provides Iran's hardliners ammunition to exit deal.

Day 44. Trump told Netanyahu 'low key' — first signal of friction over Lebanon strikes threatening Islamabad talks. Shift from full alignment ('separate skirmish') to implicit constraint. Vance's offer to 'restrain Lebanon strikes' not honored by Israel. US structurally unable to enforce ceasefire compliance on ally.

Day 44. Nabatieh strike killed 13 Lebanese State Security at government building — targeting expanded from Hezbollah to Lebanese government forces. Continued strikes Aita al-Shaab, al-Majadel, Hanine. HRW: strikes killed hundreds, vital bridge damaged. IDF halted 10km south of Litani, 'not planning to advance deeper.' IDF 'stretched to breaking point' (Haaretz).

Day 44. Escalation both sides. Israel: Nabatieh 13 State Security killed. Hezbollah: 100+ rockets since ceasefire, 70 on April 10, struck Ashdod Naval Base (200km — strategic depth). Haaretz: IDF assesses Hezbollah maintains 'solid military hold,' IDF 'stretched to breaking point.' Operational tempo dramatically increased on both sides.
Houthis launched two confirmed attacks on Israel within 24 hours on March 28-29. First Houthi military action against Israel in this war.

trigger_points.condition: Iran strikes Dimona nuclear reactor directly
trigger_points.mechanism: Would be extreme escalation. Could trigger nuclear contamination fears, massive US response, oil spike beyond $130.
